MXL.headline: MaxLinear Stock Rockets After Q1 Earnings: Here's Why
MXL.description: MaxLinear reported Q1 earnings of $0.22 per share, beating the Street estimate of $0.18, with revenue of $137.19 million exceeding the $135 million consensus. The company's infrastructure segment grew over 130% year-over-year, driven by strong optical data center connectivity products for hyperscale AI customers. MaxLinear stock surged 27% in after-hours trading. The company provided Q2 guidance of $160-170 million in revenue, significantly ahead of analyst expectations of $137.45 million.

INTC.headline: Why Intel Stock Skyrocketed Today
INTC.description: Intel's stock surged 23.6% after reporting strong Q1 earnings that beat Wall Street expectations. The chipmaker's data center and AI division revenue jumped 22% to $5.1 billion, with adjusted net income surging 156% to $1.5 billion. Intel is capitalizing on rising demand for CPUs driven by agentic AI, with new partnerships from Google, Tesla, and other tech leaders expected to fuel further growth.

INTC.headline: Stock Market Today, April 24: S&P 500 and Nasdaq Set New Highs on Tech Surge
INTC.description: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq reached record highs on April 24, 2026, driven by a powerful tech and chip sector rally. Intel surged 23% following a strong earnings report, while mega-cap AI leaders like Nvidia, Microsoft, and Meta extended gains. The Dow Jones slipped slightly amid pressure on cyclical stocks. The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index gained 4.32% and is on an 18-day winning streak. A DOJ decision to drop its investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell also supported market sentiment.

INTC.headline: Stock Market Today, April 24: Nvidia Surges on Soaring AI Chip Demand
INTC.description: Nvidia surged 4.3% to $208.24 on April 24, 2026, driven by soaring global AI chip demand and reaching all-time highs with a valuation above $5 trillion. The semiconductor sector rallied broadly, with AMD and Intel also posting significant gains. Nvidia is expected to report fiscal Q1 2027 earnings on May 20 with anticipated triple-digit year-over-year earnings growth and 77% revenue growth guidance.

INTC.headline: Intel Stock Hits All-Time Highs: Is the Turnaround Priced In?
INTC.description: Intel surged over 20% following strong quarterly earnings, breaking above dot-com era all-time highs with a 100%+ year-to-date gain. While the company delivered impressive results with improving AI demand and operational execution, analysts question whether the rapid rally has already priced in the turnaround, with the stock's technical indicators showing overbought conditions. The company still faces execution risks in its foundry business and competitive challenges in AI, suggesting patient in

INTC.headline: Nasdaq 100 Hits Best 4-Week Rally Since 2020 As Chips Notch Record Streak: The Week On Wall Street
INTC.description: The Nasdaq 100 achieved its strongest 4-week rally since April 2020, driven by a semiconductor surge following the extended U.S.-Iran ceasefire. Chip stocks including AMD, Intel, and Texas Instruments posted exceptional gains, with AMD up 24.6% in five days and Intel surging over 80% for the month. The rally reflects accelerating AI infrastructure spending despite geopolitical tensions and elevated gas prices impacting consumer sentiment.

INTC.headline: Intel Earnings Blowout Raises Questions Around a 117x Forward P/E
INTC.description: Intel surged 21.91% following a massive Q1 earnings beat with adjusted EPS of $0.29 vs. $0.01-0.02 consensus and revenue of $13.58B vs. $12.36B expected. The Data Center and AI segment drove results with 30.5% operating margins (up 1,600 bps YoY) and 22% revenue growth. Management guided Q2 revenue to $13.8-14.8B and EPS to $0.20, signaling sustained momentum. However, the 117x forward P/E raises valuation concerns, and the foundry segment continues posting significant losses despite technical p

INTC.headline: Two Markets In One—Chips And Oil Are Fighting
INTC.description: The market is split between AI-driven semiconductor strength and oil price pressures. Intel's strong guidance and AMD's gains are driving an 18-session winning streak in chip stocks, while Brent crude above $104 is creating margin pressure across airlines, logistics, and consumer staples. The Fed decision on April 29, coinciding with mega-cap earnings reports, will determine whether AI infrastructure momentum can overcome energy inflation concerns.

INTC.headline: The Dow Is Down While the Nasdaq-100 Soars. Blame the Chips.
INTC.description: Intel crushed Q1 2026 earnings estimates with strong AI data center orders, surging 21% to all-time highs. This sparked a semiconductor rally across the industry, with AMD up 14%, Broadcom up 11%, and Nvidia up 5%. However, the Dow lagged due to limited chip stock exposure, while the Nasdaq-100 soared 1.8%, reflecting the sector's strength in AI-driven computing demand.

INTC.headline: Nasdaq 100 Marks Fresh Records, Intel Jumps 23% On AI Chip Mania: Stock Market Today
INTC.description: The Nasdaq 100 reached fresh record highs on Friday, driven by Intel's blockbuster earnings beat that sparked a broad semiconductor rally. Intel surged 22.6% after exceeding revenue and profit forecasts, benefiting from AI server demand. The S&P 500 rose 0.7% while the Dow slipped 0.1%. Tech stocks led gains, with semiconductor ETFs posting their 18th consecutive day of gains. However, Charter Communications collapsed 23% on a subscriber miss, dragging down cable peers.

INTC.headline: Why Micron Stock Popped Today
INTC.description: Micron stock surged 4.5% following Intel's strong earnings beat, where Intel reported $0.29 per share in pro forma earnings versus expected $0.01, with $13.6 billion in sales. Intel's guidance for 5% sequential Q2 growth and shift toward AI inference and agentic tasks is expected to drive demand for Micron's high-bandwidth memory products. Micron's valuation at under 23x trailing earnings and 8x forward earnings appears attractive given its three-year track record of beating analyst forecasts.

ARM.headline: 1 No-Brainer Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock to Buy With $10,000 and Hold for the Long Term
ARM.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC) is positioned as a top AI stock for long-term investment due to its virtual monopoly in advanced semiconductor manufacturing. The article argues TSMC wins regardless of which AI chip architecture dominates—whether Nvidia's GPUs, custom ASICs, or high-performance CPUs for agentic AI. With $10,000, investors can purchase approximately 26 shares of TSMC at its current price.

ARM.headline: AMD, Arm Stocks Ride Intel's Coattails After Blowout Q1 Report
ARM.description: Intel delivered a strong Q1 earnings beat with $13.58B in revenue and 29 cents EPS, significantly exceeding analyst expectations. The company also provided robust forward guidance for Q2. This positive performance lifted semiconductor peers AMD and Arm Holdings in after-hours trading, as Intel CEO attributed gains to shifting AI infrastructure dynamics and increased demand for CPUs and advanced packaging.

GEV.headline: Got $5,000? GE Vernova Could Be the Next Great Pick-and-Shovel Stock for the Global Energy Transition
GEV.description: GE Vernova is positioned as an attractive investment for capitalizing on surging energy demand from data centers and AI infrastructure. The company raised its 2026 outlook, boasts a $150 billion backlog, and is expanding gas turbine production to 20 GW by mid-2026. It's also developing small modular reactors in partnership with Hitachi, though meaningful SMR revenue isn't expected until the 2030s.

URI.headline: Nasdaq 100 Hits Best 4-Week Rally Since 2020 As Chips Notch Record Streak: The Week On Wall Street
URI.description: The Nasdaq 100 achieved its strongest 4-week rally since April 2020, driven by a semiconductor surge following the extended U.S.-Iran ceasefire. Chip stocks including AMD, Intel, and Texas Instruments posted exceptional gains, with AMD up 24.6% in five days and Intel surging over 80% for the month. The rally reflects accelerating AI infrastructure spending despite geopolitical tensions and elevated gas prices impacting consumer sentiment.

URI.headline: Stock Market Today, April 23: Markets Dip As Strait of Hormuz Conflict Intensifies
URI.description: U.S. markets declined on April 23, 2026, with the S&P 500 falling 0.42%, Nasdaq dropping 0.89%, and the Dow dipping 0.36%. Geopolitical tensions in the Strait of Hormuz and tech sector weakness drove the decline, though some industrial and semiconductor stocks showed strength with solid earnings reports.

URI.headline: Why United Rentals Stock Jumped More Than 20% Today
URI.description: United Rentals stock surged over 20% after reporting Q1 2026 earnings that significantly beat Wall Street expectations, with revenues of $4.0 billion (up 7.2% YoY) and strong profit performance. The growth is driven by data center construction demand, along with broader commercial projects and infrastructure upgrades. CEO Matt Flannery noted that power-related projects are growing at double digits, indicating diversified demand beyond just data centers.

URI.headline: S&P 500 Pullback Reflects Valuation Pressure After Record Highs
URI.description: US equity markets pulled back Thursday morning after reaching record highs, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq declining slightly amid geopolitical tensions in the Strait of Hormuz and disappointing earnings guidance from major tech companies. IBM and ServiceNow led declines after missing on forward guidance despite beating quarterly results, while Tesla reversed early gains due to a substantial capex increase announcement. Oil prices surged on Middle East tensions, and the pullback was concentrated in

TXN.headline: Nasdaq 100 Hits Best 4-Week Rally Since 2020 As Chips Notch Record Streak: The Week On Wall Street
TXN.description: The Nasdaq 100 achieved its strongest 4-week rally since April 2020, driven by a semiconductor surge following the extended U.S.-Iran ceasefire. Chip stocks including AMD, Intel, and Texas Instruments posted exceptional gains, with AMD up 24.6% in five days and Intel surging over 80% for the month. The rally reflects accelerating AI infrastructure spending despite geopolitical tensions and elevated gas prices impacting consumer sentiment.

TXN.headline: This Old-School Tech Name Is Emerging As An Unexpected AI Winner
TXN.description: Texas Instruments (TXN) surged 19.4% after beating Q1 expectations and raising Q2 guidance, driven by unexpected strength in data center revenue which grew 90% year-over-year and now represents 11% of business. The company benefits from AI infrastructure demand through its analog semiconductors and power-conversion chips used in data centers. Multiple analysts upgraded the stock with price targets ranging from $250-$330, citing industrial resurgence, data center acceleration, and leverage from U

TXN.headline: Texas Instruments Surges 18% as Data Center Demand Spikes 90%
TXN.description: Texas Instruments stock surged 18% following strong fiscal Q1 2026 earnings driven by a 90% spike in data center demand. The company's analog products are critical for data center construction and AI connectivity. Analysts issued multiple price target increases, with consensus targets now exceeding $300, and institutional investors are accumulating shares. The company is positioned for a multiyear supercycle driven by data centers, AI, industrial, automotive, defense, and robotics applications.

TXN.headline: Stock Market Today, April 23: Markets Dip As Strait of Hormuz Conflict Intensifies
TXN.description: U.S. markets declined on April 23, 2026, with the S&P 500 falling 0.42%, Nasdaq dropping 0.89%, and the Dow dipping 0.36%. Geopolitical tensions in the Strait of Hormuz and tech sector weakness drove the decline, though some industrial and semiconductor stocks showed strength with solid earnings reports.

TXN.headline: Why Texas Instruments Rocketed Higher Today
TXN.description: Texas Instruments stock surged 19.5% after delivering strong Q1 2026 earnings that beat expectations and providing robust forward guidance. The company is benefiting from recovery in industrial and auto chips, explosive 90% growth in AI data center chips, and improved free cash flow of $4.4 billion as capital spending winds down.

TXN.headline: Stock Market Today: Dow Jones, S&P 500 Futures Slide As Iran Peace Efforts Stall— Tesla, ServiceNow, Boeing In Focus (UPDATED)
TXN.description: U.S. stock futures declined on Thursday following Wednesday's record-breaking surge, as peace efforts in the Iran war stalled amid vessel seizures in the Strait of Hormuz. Major indices fell slightly, with the Dow down 0.56%, S&P 500 down 0.38%, and Nasdaq 100 down 0.31%. Key earnings reports from Tesla, Boeing, IBM, and ServiceNow drove individual stock movements, while analysts warn of potential pullbacks as 'Magnificent Seven' firms report earnings.

IREN.headline: Stock Market Today, April 23: Iren Jumps on Ongoing Data Center Pivot
IREN.description: Iren (IREN) surged 7.50% to $52.02 on April 23, 2026, driven by positive coverage of its pivot from Bitcoin mining to AI data center operations. The company, which has grown 86% since its 2021 IPO and soared over 750% in the past year, is planning a potential $6 billion share issuance to fund its AI infrastructure expansion. However, investors remain concerned about share dilution and seek clarity on revenue projections ahead of Q3 earnings on May 7.

IREN.headline: IREN to Release Q3 FY26 Results on May 7, 2026
IREN.description: IREN Limited announced it will release its Q1 2026 financial results on May 7, 2026, followed by a conference call at 5:00 p.m. ET. The company will discuss results via live webcast and phone dial-in with a Q&A session. IREN is a vertically integrated AI Cloud provider offering data centers and GPU clusters powered by renewable energy across the U.S. and Canada.

ASTS.headline: AST SpaceMobile Is Impossible to Ignore Right Now. Here's What to Do With It.
ASTS.description: AST SpaceMobile's stock surged 280% over the past year but has fallen 33% from its 52-week high. The company is building a space-based broadband network with growing service revenues ($26.5M in 2025 vs $3.9M in 2024) and partnerships with major telecom companies. However, significant capital expenditures remain ahead for satellite launches, making profitability unlikely in the near term. The article suggests aggressive growth investors consider taking profits if they have substantial gains.

RDW.headline: 3 Space Stocks Flying Under the Radar and Worth Buying This Month
RDW.description: While most space stocks have become expensive with valuations exceeding 10x sales, the article identifies three relatively cheaper space stocks worth considering: Redwire (5.8x P/S ratio) with diverse space infrastructure products, Spire Global (below 9x sales) focusing on earth observation with improving cash burn trajectory, and newly public Arxis (9.5x P/S ratio) manufacturing space components. All three offer more affordable entry points compared to peers like Rocket Lab and Firefly Aerospac

QBTS.headline: MEXC Lists Eighteenth Batch of Ondo Tokenized Stock Trading Pairs on Spot Market
QBTS.description: MEXC has listed three new tokenized stock trading pairs in collaboration with Ondo Finance, including exposure to Unum Group, iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX), and D-Wave Quantum. The listings expand MEXC's real-world asset offerings and reflect the platform's commitment to making traditional financial assets accessible through tokenization on its spot market.

QBTS.headline: Quantum Computing Stocks IonQ, Rigetti, and D-Wave Have Soared Up to 72% in 7 Trading Sessions -- and You'll Likely Regret Chasing This Rally
QBTS.description: Quantum computing stocks IonQ, Rigetti, and D-Wave surged 72%, 37%, and 56% respectively over seven trading days, driven by an $850 billion addressable market and partnerships with Amazon. However, the article warns investors against chasing this rally, citing historical patterns of bubbles in game-changing technologies, extreme valuation multiples (P/S ratios of 106-870 vs. historical ceiling of 30-45), and the threat posed by well-capitalized competitors like Microsoft and Alphabet who have al

USAR.headline: USA Rare Earth’s $2.8B Power Play to Secure the Supply Chain
USAR.description: USA Rare Earth is executing a multi-billion-dollar strategy to build an independent rare earth elements supply chain. The company acquired Serra Verde Group's Pela Ema mine in Brazil for $2.8B, securing a revenue-generating asset with projected EBITDA of $550-650M by 2027. Additionally, USA Rare Earth is establishing a transatlantic processing hub in France through a partnership with Carester SAS, supported by the French government. The stock has surged over 50% in 30 days with analyst support,

RKLB.headline: The SpaceX IPO Is Coming. These Are the Stocks That Stand to Profit Most.
RKLB.description: With SpaceX's anticipated IPO expected to exceed $2 trillion valuation, investor enthusiasm for the space economy is expected to create a ripple effect benefiting competitors. Rocket Lab and Planet Labs are well-positioned to capitalize on this momentum, with both companies demonstrating strong financial performance and substantial backlogs.

RKLB.headline: What's Going On With Rocket Lab Stock Friday?
RKLB.description: Rocket Lab (RKLB) shares rose 2.01% in premarket trading on Friday, supported by a risk-on market tone for growth stocks. The company announced a new radiation-hardened High-Performance Star Tracker for long-duration space missions. Technical analysis shows strong bullish momentum with the stock trading 18.9% above its 20-day moving average and up 286.65% over the past 12 months. The stock carries a Buy rating with an average price target of $91.20, and earnings are scheduled for May 7, 2026.

RKLB.headline: 3 Space Stocks Flying Under the Radar and Worth Buying This Month
RKLB.description: While most space stocks have become expensive with valuations exceeding 10x sales, the article identifies three relatively cheaper space stocks worth considering: Redwire (5.8x P/S ratio) with diverse space infrastructure products, Spire Global (below 9x sales) focusing on earth observation with improving cash burn trajectory, and newly public Arxis (9.5x P/S ratio) manufacturing space components. All three offer more affordable entry points compared to peers like Rocket Lab and Firefly Aerospac

RKLB.headline: Rocket Lab Completes Second Dedicated Launch for Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA)
RKLB.description: Rocket Lab successfully launched its second dedicated mission for Japan's JAXA on April 23, 2026, deploying eight spacecraft including educational satellites, ocean-monitoring equipment, and advanced antenna technology. This marks the company's 8th launch of 2026 and 87th overall, reinforcing Electron's reliability as a preferred small launcher for national space agencies.

SMR.headline: Here's Why NuScale Power Stock Is a Buy Before Earnings on May 7
SMR.description: NuScale Power stock has declined 28% since the start of 2026, presenting a potential buying opportunity before its May 7 earnings announcement. The company specializes in small modular reactors (SMRs) positioned to capitalize on a $10 trillion nuclear energy opportunity driven by AI data center energy demands. While the technology is still early-stage with only two SMRs operational globally, the analyst recommends buying now to gain exposure to potential game-changing announcements, acknowledgin

SMR.headline: How Buying Oklo Inc. Stock Today Could 10X Your Net Worth
SMR.description: Oklo Inc., a small modular reactor (SMR) developer backed by OpenAI's Sam Altman, is positioned to capitalize on the nuclear renaissance driven by AI's massive energy demands. With a $13B market cap, the company could potentially reach a $100B valuation if SMRs capture 15% of the nuclear market by 2050. However, success depends on executing projects on time, sustained data center growth, and avoiding shareholder dilution.

SMR.headline: NuScale Power Stock Falls 70% in 6 Months. Here's Why the Nuclear Energy Company Faces Headwinds.
SMR.description: NuScale Power's stock has fallen 70% over six months despite recent rallies driven by government interest in space nuclear reactors. While the company has regulatory approval advantages and potential long-term catalysts from space contracts and data center power demands, it remains unprofitable with no commercial reactors built yet. The SMR market is still small and developing, making NuScale a speculative position requiring patience.
